feature_completeness_summary: The merged feature repairs the centralized FBattleScreen close lifecycle, closes the non-modal tactical screen correctly from File -> Quit and the title-bar X, and removes manual GUI-suite intervention through real non-modal close-vector coverage and bounded async close waiting. The remaining gap is missing end-to-end automated proof that a stack-owned modal FBattleScreen unwinds correctly through the title-bar X vector, which the governing plan expected for modal launch paths.

findings.summary: Modal title-bar close unwind is not covered end-to-end. BattleSim live coverage proves modal unwind through File -> Quit, but no live regression proves that a posted modal title-bar wxEVT_CLOSE_WINDOW closes the stack-owned battle screen and returns control to its caller.

Non-modal tactical close coverage now uses the real ID_TacticalQuit command and a posted wxEVT_CLOSE_WINDOW together with waitForTopLevelWindowClosed(...), eliminating the prior need for manual operator closure in the tactical GUI suite.

Runtime and source-contract evidence still supports the centralized close-path design: onClose(...) and onMenuQuit(...) both delegate to closeBattleScreen(...), modal EndModal(...) exits the shim loop, and the tactical close path still rejects exit(...) and ExitMainLoop().

follow_up_feature_requests: Add live GUI coverage for the modal FBattleScreen title-bar close vector so a stack-owned ShowModal() caller is proven to unwind correctly after a posted wxEVT_CLOSE_WINDOW.
assumptions: The shared onClose(...) -> closeBattleScreen(...) -> EndModal(...) flow means modal title-bar close is likely correct even though the delivered live GUI coverage does not prove it end-to-end.
The reviewer worktree head matches the merged coordination tip under review because the review branch was created from the merged coordination branch after both subtasks completed.
